steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both texts criticize current scholarship on a literary period. The question asks what the authors would agree on. Look for a claim about scholarship's limitations that both texts support.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'scholars should move beyond stylistic analysis entirely': Neither text calls for abandoning style as a lens — both stay focused on stylistic patterns. ✗
  • Choice B — 'without a shift in focus, scholarship's vision will continue to be unnecessarily limited': Text 1 shows that overrepresenting experimental fragmentation distorts the picture of the period; Text 2 argues the fragmentation-versus-coherence binary 'inhibits inquiry' into other patterns. Both point to the same underlying problem: scholarship cannot see the full picture, and nothing will change without a reorientation. ✓
  • Choice C — 'scholars have misread the relationship between fragmentation and coherence': Text 1 doesn't claim scholars misread that relationship — it claims they overrepresent one style. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the period is defined primarily by its experimental techniques': Both texts push back against that narrow framing rather than endorsing it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
